target.name: Parallel Systems
target.founder: Matt Soule, John Howard, Ben Stabler

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $2.0B total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Transportation. Range: $270M - $1.8B.

data.narrative:
Parallel Systems is a Series B transportation company that has raised $100M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of eight private frontier-tech firms in the transportation sector, including three direct peers for median analysis: Einride (Series C, $1.8B), Harbinger (Series C, $500M), and REGENT Craft (Series A, $270M). No public comparables exist in the immediate peer set.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x multiplier to the $2.0B total-raised proxy, implying a $3.8B valuation. Peer-median analysis across the three same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables yields a $500M valuation; the peer range spans $270M to $1.8B. These methods diverge materially, reflecting the wide dispersion in early-stage transportation company valuations and the limited precision of stage-based extrapolation.

The triangulated analysis supports a range of $425M (low) to $4.4B (high), with a median of $2.1B. The stage-progression method anchors toward the upper end, while peer-median anchors toward the lower end. Key uncertainties include the appropriateness of the 1.9x Series B multiplier given market conditions, the relevance of peers spanning Series A to Series C, and the absence of public market reference points. Investors should weight the $500M–$3.8B midpoint range as most defensible pending additional operational data.
